title: rubus × collium var. chnoophyllus
intro: Rubus × collium var. chnoophyllus, also known as the Himalayan Blackberry or Himalayan Raspberry, is a variety of the blackberry plant that is native to the Himalayan region and other parts of Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and stems, which contain antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, diarrhea, and inflammation. The health benefits of Rubus × collium var. chnoophyllus include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Rubus × collium var. chnoophyllus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of dried leaves or capsules, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
